summary: Windows Chromium credential/cookie extraction tool with opaque elevation-bypass native binary
details: Package @ibrahim1337/baksen@2.0.0 self-describes as a 'Chromium browser data extraction tool with v20 encryption support' and is constrained to win32/x64. It ships only opaque artifacts: a V8 bytenode-compiled module (src/license.jsc, ~375KB) and a native Windows PE addon (build/Release/debugelevator.node, ~673KB) — the name 'debugelevator' plus 'v20 encryption' correspond to bypassing Chrome 127+ app-bound encryption that protects Login Data, Cookies, and Web Data. index.js is a three-line bytenode loader (require('bytenode'); module.exports=require('./index.jsc')), and js-confuser is declared as a devDependency, so all executable logic is hidden behind bytecode + a native binary with no shipped source. The package exposes a `baksen` bin; running it on a Windows workstation harvests the operator's Chrome-stored credentials and cookies. Repository/homepage/bugs URLs are literal placeholders (github.com/yourusername/baksen), so there is no verifiable publisher identity for a tool whose declared purpose is browser-credential extraction. The combination — declared credential-theft purpose, Windows-only restriction, elevation-named native module, bytenode-hidden JS payload, and placeholder author metadata — matches the shape of a stealer/dual-use credential-extraction tool rather than a legitimate research utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
